pages.raw_text:
Ask of ]. Il. ONO, of DR.. Allen, of
host of others how they were knitted
in that convention after depending
upon solemn promises. Even Ed
Sanders can tell you things of how
he was politically murdered by the
corporation bandits.


And now scan the list of men who
are working as managers of the
Spokesman eview-Acuff campaign,


Do you find there any of the old
workers. the old trusted leaders of
republcanism?


Instead you find new machine, q
powerful machine, being built up by
Charles p. Lund, dictated by the
Spokesman-Review and the corpora
lions.


Where is d. r. Ham, w. s. McCrea
Horace kimball, Fred Dudley, the
Crows and a host of other regular
workers?


They have all been passed up by
Lund at the orders of the Spokesman
Review, the Washington Water Power
company and the Pacific States Tele-
phone company. Not man of them
is taking part in the campaign work


Yet the Spokesman-Revie is tell
ing you how harmony in the demo-
cratic ranks has fed before a ma
chine. Floyd Daggett was the unani-
nous choice of the peoples conven-
ton. There was no other candidate


w. Fl. Acuff was nominated by the
Ennly q m A
by means of trickery, by the slaugh-
ter of unsuspecting stalwart republic
cans, by the sacrifice of honest indi-
vidual candidates.


Which shows more the marks of
the machine rule?


And the treacherous work of the
corporation machine has not ceased.


Aside from the temerarious pledges
by Acuff to the churches. to the sa-
loon element and to the athletic clubs
of the city. all of which are contra
dictory and insincere. other honest
candidates on the socalled republican
ticket are being knitted.


Wiscomb for city treasurer and
Michaels for city comptroller are be-
ing traded in order to bring about the
election of certain councilmen who
will go into the council wearing the
collars of the Washington Water
Power company and the telephone
company.


Such men as Jack Snyder, the no.
torious whereas" candidate. owned
and controlled by he telephone COM
pany; J. S. Phillips. the Washington
Water Power company's employee, and
other councilmanic candidates to
whom attention will be paid later, are
to be elected at the sacrifice even of
the comptroller and treasurer.


And Charlie Lund and his corpora
tion campaign committee dare not
deny this statement.


Such black treachery, such cold
blooded manipulation to bring about
